summary.text: Historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP. Several fund data points are also provided by Finnhub.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI AC World index. The fund tracks a market cap-weighted index of large- and mid-cap global stocks, covering 85% of the developed and emerging markets capitalization. ACWI was launched on Mar 26, 2008 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Investors piled into ETFs at a record pace in the first half of 2026. Here's where their money is flowing.
data.text: Investors have poured money into exchange-traded funds at a rapid pace in the first half of 2026, demonstrating an unrelenting appetite for stocks associated with the AI theme.

data.title: ETF Edge: A fundamental shift in international investing as geopolitical concerns swing markets
data.text: Political and economic tensions, domestic headlines, currency flows and the Federal Reserve have all combined to reignite the “United States vs. international” investing debate.

data.title: The Global Economy Is in for a World of Hurt. How to Find Profits.
data.text: A more balanced portfolio can handle somber U.S. equity performance, as long as strong equity returns from abroad continue.

data.title: Citi Research: AI Stocks Have Driven 50% of MSCI’s World Index Gains
data.text: This year, the artificial intelligence theme has driven nearly half of the MSCI All Country World Index’s (NASDAQ:ACWI) 11% return, according to a Citi Research report. Stocks with high AI…

data.title: China-focused ETFs fall after country's growth disappoints, deepening 2023 losses
data.text: Exchange-traded funds that target Chinese stocks were falling Monday, after China reported disappointing growth for the second quarter, bringing the ETFs deeper into the red so far this year.

holdings.infoBox: ACWI is an equity ETF with a total of 2280 individual holdings. The top holdings are Apple stock at 4.80%, NVIDIA at 4.70%, Microsoft at 2.77%, Amazon.com at 2.37%, and Alphabet at 2.02%.
